appeals.resolvedAdjusted: Grade adjusted based on additional criteria.

landing.heroDescription: Submissions, peer review, rubrics, and grades — all in one place.
landing.introTitle: Peerly — a new player in peer assessment
landing.feature1Desc: With Peerly you can upload, launch, conduct, complete, and export assignment reports. The functionality can also be connected as a plugin to various LMS.
landing.feature2Desc: Peer review develops students' practical skills and reduces the teacher's workload, eliminating the need for individual review of every submission.
landing.feature3Desc: Reviewer reassignment, reviewer control, anonymity settings, and other features — benefiting both teachers and students.
landing.rolesDesc: In Peerly, users are divided into three main groups, each with their own set of available actions.
landing.roleStudentsDesc: The app design accounts for multiple concurrent assignments across different subjects with different statuses and submission formats.
landing.roleTeachersDesc: The teacher interface allows configuring, tracking, and exporting assignments, as well as monitoring individual student actions.
landing.roleAdminDesc: The administrator can monitor system health and metrics.
landing.ctaTitle: Peerly will help optimize teacher workload and improve the quality of education.

help.title: Help & Support
help.subtitle: Find answers to frequently asked questions or contact support
help.noAnswerFound: Didn't find the answer to your question?
help.chatSupportDesc: Contact us via chat and we'll help you in real time.
help.contactInstructorDesc: Contact your instructor or course administrator for help. They can answer questions related to your assignments and courses.
help.contactInstructorHint: Use email or the course announcement system to contact your instructor

faq.gs1q: How do I sign in to Peerly?
faq.gs1a: Go to the sign-in page and enter your email and password. If you're a new user, you need to register first or receive an invitation from your instructor.
faq.gs2q: Where can I find my course list?
faq.gs2a: After signing in, go to the "Courses" section via the sidebar menu. There you'll see all courses you participate in as a student or teacher.
faq.gs3q: How do I submit an assignment?
faq.gs3a: Open the desired course, select an assignment from the list. Click "Submit Work" and upload your file or enter text in the corresponding field. Make sure you follow the format and deadline.
faq.gs4q: How do I review another student's work?
faq.gs4a: In the "Reviews" section, you'll see works assigned to you for review. Open the work, study the material, and fill in the grading rubric. Leave a constructive comment and submit the review.
faq.gs5a: A rubric is a set of criteria for evaluating work. Each criterion has a specific number of points. The teacher creates rubrics for each assignment to ensure objective evaluation.
faq.gs6q: How do I view my grades?
faq.gs6a: Go to the "Gradebook" section in the sidebar menu. There you'll see all grades across all courses, including peer-review grades and final grades from the teacher.
faq.ts1q: I can't sign in. What should I do?
faq.ts1a: Make sure you're entering the correct email and password. Check if Caps Lock is on. If you forgot your password, use the "Recover Password" function on the sign-in page. If the problem persists, contact the administrator.
faq.ts2q: I don't see my course in the list
faq.ts2a: Check that you're enrolled in the course. Contact your instructor to make sure you've been added to the participant list. Try refreshing the page or signing out and back in.
faq.ts3q: File won't upload when submitting work
faq.ts3a: Check the file size (no more than 50 MB) and format (usually PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT are allowed). Make sure you have a stable internet connection. Try a different browser or clear the cache.
faq.ts4q: An assignment disappeared from my list
faq.ts4a: The teacher may have changed the visibility settings or the assignment deadline. Check the "Completed Assignments" section or contact your instructor for clarification.
faq.ts5q: I didn't receive the work assigned to me for review
faq.ts5a: Work distribution happens automatically after the submission deadline. If more than 24 hours have passed and there's still no work, contact your instructor.
faq.ts6a: Make sure you clicked "Save" or "Submit" after writing the comment. Check your internet connection and try again. Copy the text to the clipboard just in case.
faq.cs1q: How do I contact technical support?
faq.cs1a: You can contact support via the chat in the bottom-right corner of the screen (if available) or reach out to your instructor/administrator for help.
faq.cs2q: Where do I report a system bug?
faq.cs2a: If you found a bug, describe the problem in as much detail as possible (what you were doing, what error occurred, screenshots) and submit it via the feedback form or write to your instructor.

terms.title: Terms & Policy
terms.terms1Text: By using the Peerly platform, you agree to these terms of use. If you do not agree with these terms, please do not use the service.
terms.terms2Text: Peerly provides a platform for peer review of student work. You agree to:
terms.terms2Item1: Use the service only for educational purposes
terms.terms2Item3: Not upload materials that violate copyright
terms.terms2Item5: Treat other students' work with respect
terms.terms3Text: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials. Report any unauthorized use of your account immediately. Providing false information during registration may result in account suspension.
terms.terms4Text: You retain all rights to your uploaded content. By uploading work, you grant a limited license for use of the materials within the learning and review process of your course.
terms.terms5Text: The service is provided "as is." We are not responsible for the accuracy of grades or reviews provided by other users. Final grades are determined by the course instructor.
terms.terms6Text: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. By continuing to use the service after changes, you accept the new terms.
terms.privacy1Item1: Name, email, and academic information during registration
terms.privacy3Text: Uploaded works are visible only to the course instructor and students assigned for review (according to assignment anonymity settings). Instructors have access to all works and reviews in their courses.
terms.privacy4Text: We store your data during active use of the service and for a reasonable period after course completion for archival purposes. You can request deletion of your data at any time.
terms.privacy5Text: We apply modern security measures to protect your data, including encryption in transit and at rest, access control, and regular security audits.
terms.privacy6Item1: Obtain a copy of your personal data
terms.privacy6Item3: Request data deletion (subject to academic requirements)
terms.contactText: For questions about terms of use and privacy policy, contact us:
terms.contactResponseTime: We aim to respond to all inquiries within 2-3 business days.

status.title: Service Status
status.subtitle: Current state of Peerly systems and incident history
status.degradedDesc: Some features may be slower than usual
status.outageDesc: We are working on restoring the service
status.subscribeDesc: Receive notifications about service status and planned maintenance
status.incident1Summary: Peerly systems are operating in normal mode.
status.incident1Update1: All services are running stably. No issues detected.
status.incident2Summary: Users experienced delays when uploading large submission files.
status.incident2Update1: Issue resolved. We increased file storage server throughput. All uploads are working normally.
status.incident2Update2: We applied a temporary fix and are monitoring the system. Upload speed has improved.
status.incident2Update3: We received reports of slow file uploads and began investigating.
status.incident3Summary: Some users could not sign in due to authentication server issues.
status.incident3Update1: Authentication service fully restored. All users can sign in.
status.incident3Update2: We restarted the authentication service. Most users can sign in, continuing to monitor.
status.incident3Update3: We are investigating sign-in issues reported by users.
status.incident4Summary: Scheduled database update with brief service interruption.
status.incident4Update1: Maintenance completed successfully. All systems are operating normally.
status.incident4Update2: Scheduled maintenance has started. Service will be unavailable until 04:00 UTC.
status.incident5Summary: Teachers received errors when attempting to export gradebook to CSV format.
status.incident5Update1: Export module bug fixed. Grade export function is working correctly.
status.incident5Update2: We received reports of export errors and began investigating.

peerSession.distributionManualDesc: You assign submissions to students manually. Maximum control.
peerSession.anonymityFullDesc: Students cannot see the author or reviewer. Recommended for objectivity.
peerSession.anonymityPartialDesc: The author is hidden, but the reviewer is visible. Useful for learning to review.
peerSession.anonymityNoneDesc: Everyone can see each other. Suitable for small group work.
peerSession.reassignmentHint: Students can request a different submission to review if the current one is too difficult or there is a conflict of interest.
peerSession.reassignmentDeadlineHint: After this time, reassignment will be unavailable
peerSession.tipText: For courses with many students (30+), random distribution with full anonymity and k=3-5 reviews is recommended.

plugins.title: Plugins and checks
plugins.subtitle: Configure automated checks to improve submission quality
plugins.plagiarismDesc: Automatic check for borrowings and matches
plugins.plagiarismThresholdHint: Submissions with matches above {{threshold}}% will be flagged for review
plugins.linterDesc: Code check for standards compliance and best practices
plugins.linterHint: Linting results will be shown to students before submission
plugins.formatCheckHint: Files in other formats will be rejected during upload
plugins.anonymizationDesc: Automatic removal of names and personal data from submissions
plugins.anonymizationDetails: The system will automatically replace student names and other identifiers with anonymous codes before sending for review.
plugins.allOptionalText: You can enable or disable any checks. Settings can also be changed after publishing the assignment.

publish.title: Ready to publish
publish.subtitle: Review settings and publish the assignment or save as draft
publish.warningText: After publishing, the assignment will be visible to students. You can edit settings, but some changes (e.g., deadlines) may affect work already in progress.
publish.draftsInfo: Drafts are saved automatically and are only visible to you.
publish.publishedInfo: Published assignments are immediately visible to course students.

participantImport.title: Import Participants
participantImport.generateInviteDesc: Generate one-time invite codes for self-registration

assignmentPicker.title: Attach rubric to assignment
assignmentPicker.selectFromList: Select an assignment from the list above

rubricCriterion.commentPlaceholder: Leave a comment for this criterion

comparison.title: Version Comparison
comparison.changesLegend: — changes compared to the other version

validationChecks.willRunAfterUpload: Checks will run after file upload

createRule.ruleWillAlwaysFire: Rule will always fire on trigger
createRule.priorityHint: Rules with lower numbers execute first
createRule.ruleStartsAfterSave: Rule will start working after saving
createRule.configureTriggerAndActions: Configure trigger and actions to preview the rule

addExtension.cannotChangeStudent: Student cannot be changed after creation

assignment.teacherReviewingWork: The teacher is reviewing your work and reviews
assignment.dontForgetSubmit: Don't forget to submit the final version before the deadline
assignment.contactTeacher: Contact the teacher for an individual deadline
